meta.title: The Marian Dogmas | Protesting or Catholic Ep 4
meta.author: Canon Press

questions.question: According to the episode, one of the biggest practical differences between Protestants and Roman Catholics is how they relate to whom?
options: Mary, the mother of our Lord
questions.answer: Mary, the mother of our Lord

questions.question: The episode claims the rosary prayed daily by faithful Catholics is primarily directed to whom?
questions.answer: Mary

questions.question: In the episode’s ordering, which Marian dogma is described as the least objectionable for most historic Protestants?
options: Mary as Theotokos (Mother of God / God-bearer)
questions.answer: Mary as Theotokos (Mother of God / God-bearer)

questions.question: The Council of Ephesus (431), as summarized in the episode, affirmed Mary as Theotokos against which opponents?
questions.answer: Nestorians

questions.question: Perpetual virginity, as described, includes which additional claim about Mary’s birth experience?
options: That she bore Jesus without pain
That she remained a physically intact virgin during and after giving birth (postpartum virginity)
That she conceived Jesus by Joseph
That she never traveled to Egypt
questions.answer: That she remained a physically intact virgin during and after giving birth (postpartum virginity)

questions.question: Which Gospel verse does the episode cite as sounding strange if Mary never had marital relations with Joseph after Jesus’ birth?
questions.answer: Matthew 1:25

questions.question: Which second-century text does the episode associate with perpetual virginity’s stranger details?
questions.answer: The Protoevangelium of James

questions.question: The Immaculate Conception, as defined in the episode, teaches what about Mary?
options: That Jesus was conceived by the Holy Spirit
That Mary was conceived without original sin and lived a sinless life (as presented)
That Mary ascended bodily into heaven
That Mary remained a virgin only until Jesus’ birth
questions.answer: That Mary was conceived without original sin and lived a sinless life (as presented)

questions.question: Which medieval Franciscan theologian does the episode credit with helping popularize the Immaculate Conception in the 13th century?
questions.answer: Duns Scotus

questions.question: When does the episode say the Assumption of Mary was declared a dogma for all Catholics?
The episode links an early attestation of Mary’s “repose” tradition to which kind of text?
options: A heretical gnostic text called the Book of Mary’s Repose
questions.answer: A heretical gnostic text called the Book of Mary’s Repose

questions.question: Which Marian title does the episode list as something many hope the pope would dogmatize (co-redeemer with Christ)?
questions.answer: Co-redemptrix

questions.question: According to the episode, Protestants and Catholics agree public revelation to the whole church ended when?
options: At the death of the last apostle
questions.answer: At the death of the last apostle

questions.question: Which justification strategy does the episode associate with publications like Catholic Answers?
options: Explicit presence in unwritten oral traditions from the apostles
questions.answer: Explicit presence in unwritten oral traditions from the apostles

questions.question: The episode argues typological Marian readings fail chiefly because typology is what?
options: Often ambiguous and insufficient for dogmatic specifics
questions.answer: Often ambiguous and insufficient for dogmatic specifics

questions.question: Which Pauline point does the episode use to argue Mary was a sinner like others?
questions.answer: Romans 3:23’s “all have sinned” line (as summarized)

questions.question: What does the episode claim about Paul’s letters regarding Mary?
options: Paul never mentions Mary by name
Paul teaches the Immaculate Conception explicitly
questions.answer: Paul never mentions Mary by name

questions.question: Justin Martyr is cited as emphasizing which exclusivity about Jesus?
options: Jesus is the only blameless and righteous man (as paraphrased)
questions.answer: Jesus is the only blameless and righteous man (as paraphrased)

questions.question: Clement of Alexandria is quoted to contrast sinlessness with what being “natural and common to all”?
questions.answer: Sin

questions.question: Basil the Great is used to argue Mary needed redemption from what sin at the cross?
options: The sin of doubt (as summarized from Basil’s reading of Simeon’s sword)
The sin of idolatry toward Jesus
The sin of denying the resurrection
questions.answer: The sin of doubt (as summarized from Basil’s reading of Simeon’s sword)

questions.question: Tertullian is cited as treating Mary in Matthew 12 as a figure for what?
questions.answer: The unbelieving synagogue / Jews (as summarized)

questions.question: Origen’s argument about scandal at the passion is used to conclude Mary did what?
options: Was scandalized and needed redemption like others (as summarized)
questions.answer: Was scandalized and needed redemption like others (as summarized)

questions.question: Regarding Mary’s end, what does the episode claim Epiphanius actually says?
options: No one knows what happened to Mary (as summarized)
questions.answer: No one knows what happened to Mary (as summarized)

questions.question: Which pope’s 1953 quotation does the episode use to illustrate extreme Marian praise language?
questions.answer: Pius XII

questions.question: The episode’s closing worry about lay Catholics confusing Mary with the Trinity blames what combination?
options: Bad catechesis combined with practices that render divine-like honors to Mary (as summarized)
questions.answer: Bad catechesis combined with practices that render divine-like honors to Mary (as summarized)
